differential_diagnoses: Portal hypertensive biliopathy leading to hemobilia
Portal vein stenosis/thrombosis due to prior biliary surgery
next_steps: Perform diagnostic angiography with possible embolization
Consult interventional radiology for portal pressure assessment and potential TIPS or shunt intervention
Repeat EGD focusing on portal hypertensive changes and possible source of hematobilia
Tissue sampling (ERCP/brushing or biopsy) for ruling out malignancy in the hilar region
Multidisciplinary review by gastroenterology, hepatology, and hepatobiliary surgery

differential_diagnoses: Hemobilia secondary to portal hypertensive biliopathy
Hepatic artery pseudoaneurysm (complication of prior biliary reconstruction)
next_steps: Formal selective hepatic artery angiography to rule out pseudoaneurysm
Measurement of serum tumor markers including CA 19-9 and CEA
Percutaneous transhepatic cholangiography (PTC) for evaluation of the biliary-vascular interface and potential tissue sampling
Multidisciplinary hepatobiliary surgery and interventional radiology consultation for management of portal vein stenosis and biliary access
Endoscopic surveillance for active hemobilia or evidence of blood originating from the biliary-enteric anastomosis
